body_txt: A Non-Action Guy is a male who is not heavily endowed with a certain quality that's expected of males in action oriented shows. More specifically, he's just not a fighter. Maybe he's a Lovable Coward or Tagalong Kid , but for whatever reason he's useless in a fight. He's usually a sidekick ; often to the Action Girl (or Action Girlfriend ), or sometimes to an entire Five-Man Band (or Battle Harem ). Expect him to need occasional rescuing . This last portion has the added danger of emasculating him or just making him annoying in the eyes of viewers. Note this isn't always a bad thing , as realistically The Team needs specialists in all fields. The danger of alienating viewers tends to come if the Non-Action Guy is either a Vanilla Protagonist surrounded by much more interesting characters, is always needing to be bailed out by the Action Girl cast members, or The Load that just eats up screentime that could be better spent elsewhere. Side or supporting characters rarely get this kind of flak, it's usually the male protagonist specifically. The Non-Action Guy seems much more likely to be played for comedy than female characters who don't fight. While women are not expected to kick ass (and thus those that do are exceptional), men often are—so a Non-Action Guy's lack of ability is written as something to laugh at. The closest female equivalents would be either the Faux Action Girl or Neutral Female but they rarely get as much vitriol as a "useless" male. Often he'll either grow into a more action-oriented role, become ignored , or become the Butt-Monkey as punishment for his failure at masculinity . A common, more acceptable practice is to put him on the Mission Control (of the Female Fighter, Male Handler sort), where he can be of actual help without having to use any sort of action skill. To avoid the negative connotations associated with this character type, it might be combined with other tropes such as the Loveable Rogue or The Smart Guy . Such a character may be unsuitable for action due to age, build, infirm, upbringing, or simply because it would be a waste of his talent. However, they make up for it by being a Guile Hero , The Chessmaster , or The Strategist . It sometimes goes hand in hand with Con Men Hate Guns or Thou Shalt Not Kill . The Non Action guy on a team may not literally be "no-action", but they may be the weakest member as far as offensive power is concerned. But it's not really what they are there to do . They may make up for it by using purely psychological tactics. They may also be a Terror Hero or a Master of Illusion , deception, or any various methods of combat on the cerebral level. Other times they're a healer. Very often, either a Gentleman Detective or Gentleman Thief . In all other cases, he's Weak, but Skilled . Note this trope should only apply to shows or settings with a lot of fighters or a heavy focus on action, except for this one male. If the show is a nonviolent Slice of Life where no one is fighting then someone being bad at fighting doesn't stand out. If he's the protagonist, then he's likely an Action Survivor . If he's the Villain, he's likely a Non-Action Big Bad . In RPGs , he will often be the Utility Party Member . Compare Unfazed Everyman .
